requirements.user_story: As a developer using NoPilot, I want a persistent multi-layered project profile stored in .nopilot/profile/, so that project context survives across sessions and features.

acceptance_criteria.ears: THE profile directory SHALL contain four independent layer files: l0-infra.json, l1-arch.json, l2-decisions.json, l3-status.json
THE L2 decisions layer SHALL be physically separated from L0/L1/L3 and independently disableable via feature flag
THE profile files SHALL NOT be tracked by git (.nopilot/ added to .gitignore)
THE profile read/write interface SHALL be decoupled from trigger timing, so that v4 dreaming can replace the trigger without changing storage format
EACH layer file SHALL conform to a corresponding JSON Schema in schemas/profile-l{0-3}.schema.json
EACH profile layer file SHALL include an updated_at ISO 8601 timestamp, written on every profile write operation

downstream_impact.tech_implications: 4 JSON Schema files, 1 read/write interface module

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want the profile to be automatically updated after every build completes, so that project memory stays current without manual effort.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN build completes successfully, THE SYSTEM SHALL extract information from discover.json, spec.json, build_report.json, and decisions.json and write to the corresponding profile layers
THE writer SHALL update both greenfield and feature mode builds
WHILE the L2 feature flag is disabled, THE writer SHALL skip writing to l2-decisions.json
WHEN writing to an existing profile, THE writer SHALL merge incrementally, not overwrite (e.g., new entities added to domain model, not replacing it)
THE writer SHALL NOT modify any existing specs/ artifacts during the write process

downstream_impact.tech_implications: build.md appends profile write step at end
downstream_impact.test_complexity: Medium — must verify correct extraction from multiple artifact formats

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want discover/spec/build stages to read the profile and inject it as context, so that each stage is aware of the existing project.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN a stage starts and profile exists, THE SYSTEM SHALL read relevant profile layers and inject as context
WHILE profile does not exist, THE SYSTEM SHALL proceed without profile context (graceful degradation)
THE profile read operation SHALL complete within 2 seconds

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Each command prompt adds profile reading logic

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want NoPilot to automatically detect whether I'm starting a new project or adding to an existing one, so that I get the right workflow without manual configuration.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN /discover starts, THE SYSTEM SHALL check two dimensions: profile existence and codebase existence (non-empty directory with git history or dependency files)
WHEN no code and no profile detected, THE SYSTEM SHALL enter greenfield mode
WHEN code exists but no profile detected, THE SYSTEM SHALL trigger first-time onboarding: scan codebase to generate L0/L1/L3 profile, then ask user to choose greenfield or feature mode
WHEN profile exists, THE SYSTEM SHALL prompt user to choose greenfield or feature mode
THE selected mode SHALL be written to a context variable accessible by all subsequent stages

downstream_impact.tech_implications: discover.md entry logic restructured with two-dimensional detection

requirements.user_story: As a developer in feature mode, I want each discover/spec/build stage to automatically adjust its behavior (skip, inherit, or append), so that I don't repeat already-decided work.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHILE mode=feature, discover Layer 1 (direction selection) SHALL be skipped
WHILE mode=feature, discover Layer 2 SHALL inherit tech stack, NFR, and design philosophy from profile, and only collect feature-incremental definitions
WHILE mode=feature, design philosophy extraction SHALL be skipped; conflicts with profile philosophy SHALL be detected and reported (see REQ-007)
WHILE mode=feature, discover Layer 3 acceptance criteria SHALL include regression guard EARS clauses (see REQ-009)
WHILE mode=feature, spec phase SHALL read existing codebase to avoid interface conflicts
WHILE mode=feature, build phase SHALL create worktrees branching from existing code, not empty branches
WHILE mode=greenfield, THE SYSTEM SHALL continue to execute all stages identically to the current behavior, with the sole addition of profile writing after build

downstream_impact.tech_implications: discover.md, spec.md, build.md each gain mode branches

requirements.user_story: As a developer adding a feature, I want the system to scan my codebase for current structure information, so that feature design is grounded in actual code state.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN feature discover starts, THE SYSTEM SHALL scan the codebase to extract: tech stack, directory structure, module dependencies, test coverage summary

WHILE codebase file count exceeds a configurable threshold (default: 500 files, configurable via .nopilot/config.json), THE SYSTEM SHALL split scanning across sub-agents per top-level directory and merge results
THE scan results SHALL supplement (not replace) profile L0/L1 data

downstream_impact.tech_implications: New scanning logic, possibly file analysis tooling

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want the system to detect when my feature requirements conflict with existing project decisions, so that I can make an informed choice to adapt or evolve.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN feature requirements are defined, THE SYSTEM SHALL compare them against profile L2 design philosophy, constraints, and architecture decisions
WHEN a conflict is detected, THE SYSTEM SHALL present the conflict with three options: ADAPT (fit within existing architecture), EVOLVE (upgrade architecture), DEFER (move feature to V2)
THE conflict report SHALL include the specific profile entry that conflicts and the specific feature requirement
WHEN user chooses EVOLVE, THE SYSTEM SHALL record the architecture evolution decision in the feature's discover.json and flag it for profile update after build

downstream_impact.tech_implications: LLM semantic comparison, depends on profile L2 quality
downstream_impact.test_complexity: Medium — need conflict and non-conflict test cases

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want new feature entities and relationships to be merged with the existing domain model, so that the project's domain model evolves coherently.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN feature discover defines new entities or relationships, THE SYSTEM SHALL present them alongside the existing domain model from the profile
WHEN a new entity name collides with an existing one, THE SYSTEM SHALL flag it as a conflict for user resolution
WHEN build completes, THE profile writer SHALL merge the feature's domain model additions into the profile L3 domain model snapshot

requirements.user_story: As a developer adding a feature, I want acceptance criteria to automatically include regression guards, so that existing behavior is explicitly protected.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHILE mode=feature, Layer 3 SHALL automatically generate at least one regression_guard type EARS criterion per requirement that touches existing modules
EACH regression guard SHALL reference the specific existing behavior being protected
THE regression guard generation SHALL NOT alter the format or structure of existing EARS acceptance criteria types

requirements.user_story: As a developer adding a frontend feature, I want UI design to follow the existing design system rather than exploring new styles, so that visual consistency is maintained.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHILE mode=feature AND product has frontend, UI Taste phase SHALL switch from exploration (creativeRange: REIMAGINE) to adherence (creativeRange: REFINE)
WHEN entering UI Taste in feature mode, THE SYSTEM SHALL read existing design system from profile ui_taste.designDNA or scan for CSS/Tailwind/design-tokens
THE SYSTEM SHALL continue to use exploration mode (REIMAGINE) when mode=greenfield

downstream_impact.tech_implications: taste-orchestrator.ts already supports creativeRange; connect mode signal
downstream_impact.test_complexity: Low — style-detector.ts has existing test base

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want the Critic to audit which existing modules and interfaces are affected by my feature, so that integration risks are caught before build.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHILE mode=feature, Critic SHALL perform an additional impact audit after spec review: identify touched modules, changed interfaces, dependency direction changes
WHEN impact audit finds dependency direction violations or interface breaking changes, THE Critic SHALL flag them as severity: block
THE impact audit results SHALL be written to specs/features/feat-xxx/spec_review.json

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want feature artifacts stored in specs/features/feat-xxx/, so that feature-level work is isolated from project-level artifacts.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN mode=feature, ALL discover/spec/build artifacts SHALL be written to specs/features/feat-xxx/ instead of specs/
THE feature directory name SHALL be derived from user's feature description (slugified)
THE feature artifacts SHALL reference project-level profile by path, not copy its contents
THE SYSTEM SHALL continue to write greenfield artifacts to specs/ as before

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Artifact output paths parameterized in all commands

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want the system to automatically capture why architectural decisions were made, so that future features inherit the reasoning, not just the outcome.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN build completes and L2 feature flag is enabled, THE writer SHALL extract design philosophy from discover.json, decision rationale from decisions.json, and contract amendments from build_report.json, and write to l2-decisions.json
EACH L2 entry SHALL contain: the decision, the rationale (why), the alternatives considered, and the source artifact
WHILE L2 feature flag is disabled, THE SYSTEM SHALL skip all L2 read/write operations without affecting other layers
THE L2 feature flag toggle SHALL NOT affect L0, L1, or L3 profile operations

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Extract 'why' from multiple artifact types; quality depends on Agent summarization

requirements.user_story: As a developer in feature mode, I want the system to use captured architectural intent as design constraints, so that my feature aligns with the project's reasoning, not just its structure.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHILE mode=feature AND L2 exists, THE SYSTEM SHALL inject L2 content as design constraints during discover Layer 2 and conflict detection
WHILE L2 does not exist or is disabled, THE SYSTEM SHALL proceed with structural constraints only (L0/L1/L3)
THE conflict detection (REQ-007) SHALL use L2 as the primary anchor for philosophy/intent conflicts

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Connect REQ-003 reader and REQ-007 conflict detection

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want to be warned when the profile is potentially stale, so that I don't design features based on outdated context.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN profile is read and profile.updated_at is more than 24 hours older than the latest git commit timestamp (threshold configurable via .nopilot/config.json), THE SYSTEM SHALL warn the user and recommend regenerating the profile
WHEN user confirms regeneration, THE SYSTEM SHALL trigger a fresh codebase scan and update L0/L1/L3
WHEN user chooses to proceed despite warning, THE SYSTEM SHALL record the acknowledged staleness as a known risk

invariants.statement: Greenfield mode behavior SHALL remain identical to current behavior, with the sole addition of profile writing after build
Profile L2 (decisions) SHALL never block or alter system behavior when disabled
Feature-scoped artifacts SHALL reference but never copy project-level profile content
Profile write operations SHALL never modify existing specs/ artifacts
Code scanning for large codebases SHALL be bounded by configurable thresholds and parallelized via sub-agents
